howItWorks.intro: This baby formula calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: General rule: 2.5 oz per pound of body weight per day
Maximum: 32 oz/day (do not exceed without medical advice)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much formula does a baby need?
faq.answer: General guideline: 2.5 ounces per pound of body weight per day, up to 32 oz max. A 10-lb baby needs about 25 oz/day. After 6 months when solids begin, formula intake may decrease to 24-28 oz/day.

faq.question: How do I know if my baby is eating enough?
faq.answer: Signs of adequate feeding: 6+ wet diapers/day, steady weight gain (4-7 oz/week in first months), content between feedings, meeting developmental milestones. Report any concerns to your pediatrician.

faq.question: How much does formula cost per month?
faq.answer: Brand name: $150-250/month. Store brand: $100-150/month. Specialty (hypoallergenic): $200-400/month. A baby uses about 3-4 cans of powdered formula per month. Generic brands are nutritionally equivalent to name brands.
